channel.title: Claude Bar
channel.description: Auto-update feed for Claude Bar — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts.

item.title: Version 11.38
item.description:
• GitLab pipeline diagnostics — new MCP tools to find what broke a failed pipeline: cb_gitlab_get_pipeline for status + duration, cb_gitlab_list_pipeline_jobs with a scope=failed filter, and cb_gitlab_get_job_log returning the last 64 KB (configurable up to 512 KB) of a job's trace.
  • GitLab pipeline actions — gated write tools to drive pipelines: cb_gitlab_retry_pipeline, cb_gitlab_cancel_pipeline, and cb_gitlab_create_pipeline (run a pipeline on a ref).
  • Fixed GitLab connector showing "off" after Add — the Settings → MCP connectors list checked the bare gitlab Keychain slot, but multi-instance PATs live at gitlab:<id>. The row now trusts the registry mirror that gets flipped on a successful add, so GitLab flips to "connected" the moment you finish the Add Instance sheet.

item.title: Version 11.37
item.description:
• Per-account refresh — a refresh button next to each account's Web badge lets you force a fresh usage scrape for just that account, without disturbing the others or waiting for the next poll cycle.
  • Faster fresh data after launch — when claude.ai's React SPA returns 5h-only on the first scrape (a hydration race), the app now auto-retries 12 seconds later so the full 5h + 7d chart fills in within seconds instead of after the next 2-minute tick.
  • Quick Login fix — Fixed "Internal error: no store reference" right after auto-authorize. Account wiring was capturing transient @StateObject instances during App init; it now runs from a stable launch hook so weak references stay valid for the lifetime of the app.
  • Better Quick Login diagnostics — explicit log lines around the OAuth ingest path so future Quick Login failures surface the exact failing step (identity mismatch, backend ingest error, etc.) instead of going silent after the token exchange.

item.title: Version 11.36
item.description:
• Rate-limit aware polling — when claude.ai pushes back on a web usage scrape, that account backs off with exponential delays. The row badge shows the wait time, other accounts keep polling normally.
  • Popover-open boost — opening the menu triggers an immediate refresh and tightens the poll cadence for 5 minutes so the chart stays fresh while you watch. Toggle in Settings → Adaptive refresh.
  • Cookie keep-alive — a background loop pings idle web sessions every few hours so claude.ai cookies don't lapse into server-side idle timeout. Respects rate-limit backoff and the new Settings toggle.
  • Polling starts at launch — auto-swap, token refresh, and the notification handler now come up at app start instead of waiting for the first menu open. Fixes a stall after Sparkle updates.
  • Keep-alive diagnostics — Diagnostics → Web usage shows when the keep-alive last ran and how many accounts it pinged.

item.title: Version 11.35
item.description:
• Automatic credential recovery — when your active account's login expires, Claude Bar re-authenticates it in the background and swaps accounts if needed.
  • Browser-first add account — adding an account now uses an in-app browser sign-in, with the Terminal flow kept as a fallback.
  • Visible recovery — a pulsing glow marks the account being recovered, and a "Log in" button appears if it needs your input.
  • Actionable notifications — Cancel or Retry a recovery directly from its notification.
  • Auto-recovery settings — new toggle and swap / re-login delay controls under Settings → General, plus a per-account auto-authorize budget that prevents retry loops.

item.title: Version 11.34 (BETA)
item.description:
• Quick re-login is now fully automatic — one right-click signs you back in. The sheet auto-clicks Authorize on Anthropic's consent page, captures the auth code, exchanges it, and shows a clear success screen, then closes.
  • Hotfix: re-login was failing with a 400 "Invalid request format" at the token endpoint. Root cause: the OAuth authorization_code exchange body was missing the state value (the second half of the code#state string Anthropic renders). Claude Code's token endpoint requires it echoed back — without it, no tokens were ever saved.
  • Success now covers the stale "Paste this into Claude Code" page with an unmistakable confirmation instead of leaving it visible behind a one-line banner.

item.title: Version 11.33 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix: Quick re-login stalled on Anthropic's "Authentication Code" page (the one that says Paste this into Claude Code). Root cause: Claude Code CLI's OAuth client does not redirect to a machine-readable callback after the user clicks Authorize — Anthropic renders a manual-paste page instead. The Quick re-login WebView was waiting for a console.anthropic.com/oauth/code/callback?code=... URL that never fires.
  • Fix: the WebView now scans the rendered DOM for the <code>#<state> string Anthropic displays, parses it, validates the state matches the PKCE state we generated, and exchanges the code at platform.claude.com automatically. No manual copy-paste, no Terminal hop.
  • DOM scan pattern is conservative — requires ≥32-char base64url before # and ≥16-char base64url after — so transient page strings containing # (anchor URLs, error-message hashes) don't false-positive.
  • Polls every 250ms for ~5s after each page-load completion to catch React/SPA hydration of the code element. Cancels in-flight polls on subsequent navigations so they don't stack.
  • The legacy redirect-callback path is retained as a safety net in case Anthropic ever migrates the CLI client to an auto-redirect flow — not exercised today.

item.title: Version 11.32 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix: clicking Use Terminal flow instead inside the Quick re-login sheet crashed the popover with No ObservableObject of type LoginCoordinator found. The sheet declared an @EnvironmentObject dependency the floating window never populated. Re-routed the fallback through QuickReloginCoordinator.switchToTerminalFlow() so the sheet only depends on the one coordinator we explicitly inject.
  • Fix: a transient WKWebView re-navigation to the callback URL (back-button replay, redirect chain) could POST an already-consumed authorization code — the token endpoint returns 400 and the sheet surfaced .failed for a flow that had actually succeeded. The PKCE verifier and state are now consumed atomically at the top of handleRedirect so a second invocation early-exits.
  • Fix: when the per-account WKWebsiteDataStore was momentarily unresolvable (rare — registry mid-write), the WebView fell back to WKWebsiteDataStore.default() — the system-wide persistent store. claude.ai cookies could leak across every Claude Bar account on the Mac. The fallback is now .nonPersistent() so cookies die with the sheet.
  • No behaviour change for the normal happy path of the v11.30/v11.31 Quick re-login.

item.title: Version 11.31 (BETA)
item.description:
• Quick re-login auto-restarts the running `claude` and resumes the conversation. After a Quick re-login of the active account, the in-memory tokens held by the running CLI are no longer stale — Claude Bar now sends SIGINT and lets claude-watch re-launch the child with claude --resume <sessionId>, so the conversation continues without context loss.
  • Cmux panes get the same treatment. CmuxPaneRelauncher walks every active claude pane, sends Ctrl-C + claude --resume <sid>, and the user keeps their conversation across the OAuth refresh.
  • IDE windows reload if you opt in. VSCode / Cursor / Windsurf / Antigravity reload when Auto-reload IDE after swap is on, so the extension picks up the freshly-written tokens without a manual reload command.
  • Reuses the existing post-swap toggles. No new settings: the Quick re-login flow respects Auto-kill CLI sessions and Auto-reload IDE after swap. Inactive-account re-logins skip the pipeline since no running CLI is affected.

item.title: Version 11.30 (BETA)
item.description:
• Quick re-login from the right-click menu. Right-click an account → Quick re-login… drives the Claude Code OAuth flow inside an embedded WKWebView instead of bouncing through Terminal and the system browser. PKCE S256, callback intercepted at console.anthropic.com/oauth/code/callback, tokens written via a new csw ingest-oauth stdin-piped command — access tokens never appear in argv or shell history.
  • One-click Authorize for linked accounts. The new flow reuses each account's per-account WKWebsiteDataStore (the one the web-usage scraper already manages), so a user already signed in to claude.ai for usage scraping just clicks Authorize once.
  • Identity guard. The access_token JWT's email claim is decoded and compared against the account's registered email before any Keychain write. Authorising the wrong Anthropic identity at the consent screen now surfaces a "signed in as X, expected Y" banner instead of silently overwriting another account's credentials. The backend re-enforces the same check via an expectedEmail field so the guard cannot be bypassed by direct callers.
  • Terminal flow preserved as fallback. The classic claude /login path is unchanged. The new sheet exposes a Use Terminal flow instead button so a broken WebView OAuth (e.g. Anthropic alters the authorize URL) degrades to the documented recovery path without a restart.

item.title: Version 11.29 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix: after clicking Upgrade, the app was sitting on a "loading" icon for 10–20 minutes until the user clicked the menu bar to nudge it. Sparkle's installer was waiting for the host PID to exit before swapping the bundle, but the in-popover update driver passed delegate: nil to SPUUpdater and never subscribed to the restart notification — so nothing in the app actually quit.
  • Fix: the host now calls NSApp.terminate(nil) as soon as Sparkle signals install-ready. TerminationListener fires immediately, the installer swaps the bundle, and the new build relaunches without the dead-time stall.
  • Clarification on the "kills sessions" report: the upgrade does NOT SIGINT running claude CLI sessions. The local MCP gateway is briefly down during the 2–3s restart — any MCP write-tool call in flight during that window will fail, but the sessions themselves keep running and pick up the new gateway as soon as Claude Bar is back.
  • Closes #20.

item.title: Version 11.28 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix: menu-bar popover now reopens reliably after closing the Rename Account window. Previously the reopen fired on a fixed 50ms timer that raced SwiftUI's non-deterministic dismissal — when the Rename click came through the … overflow menu (which dispatches its action after its own close animation), the dismissal landed up to ~300ms later and the popover stayed permanently closed.
  • Fix: reopen now retries across the 50–500ms window after dismissal. Each attempt is a no-op while the popover is still up; the first one after the dismissal wins.
  • The matching "popover dismisses after Rename" entry has been removed from Known Issues.
  • Closes #13.

item.title: Version 11.27 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix: auto-swap no longer stalls silently when a claude session is still running. After the 60-second pending notification, the swap now fires regardless of session state. Users running claude continuously had been seeing the "Auto-swap pending" toast but no actual swap.
  • Behaviour change: the 60s grace is the entire warning window — close claude within those 60s if you want it to finish cleanly first. Auto-swap still does NOT kill the running process; enable Auto-kill CLI sessions after swap if you want claude-watch to SIGINT and restart it.
  • Notification copy updated: "Will switch to … in 60s. Close claude now if you want it to finish cleanly first."
  • Closes #18.

item.title: Version 11.26 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix: gated MCP write tools (Slack post, ClickUp comment, GitHub create issue…) called from inside a subagent or skill now surface their approval prompt. Previously every subagent gate call timed out with user_cancelled: gate timed out after 60s; direct calls from the main session worked fine.
  • Root cause: each claude session spawns its own csw mcp serve process, and Claude Code spins up extra ones for subagents. All instances raced to bind a fixed UDS path; only the winner could deliver prompts to the widget.
  • Fix: flipped the gate IPC direction. The widget proxy now listens on the UDS; every MCP server dials in as a client. Widget multiplexes N MCP-server clients onto a single stdio bridge and routes decisions back via the originating connection. Wire protocol unchanged.
  • Upgrade note: after updating, /exit any running claude sessions and relaunch — the old csw mcp serve binary still in memory uses the old IPC direction and will fight the new widget proxy until restarted.
  • Closes #21.

item.title: Version 11.25 (BETA)
item.description:
• Running claude sessions now auto-reload when MCP config changes — toggling a connector, adding a new one, or reinstalling all kick a graceful reload of every open session. No more /exit + re-launch.
  • Conversation is preserved across the reload via --resume <sid>. Uses the claude-watch wrapper Claude Bar already auto-installs in your shell rc — terminal flashes a brief ↻ Reloading claude (MCP config changed)… banner and you keep typing where you left off.
  • Install / Reinstall / Connect-token / Connect-Google now also trigger the reload — previously these paths added a connector that the running session would never see until manual restart.
  • Known edge case: the reload kills any in-flight Claude response and any text you'd typed but not yet sent. Wait for idle before toggling MCP if you're mid-stream.
  • Closes #19.

item.title: Version 11.24 (BETA)
item.description:
• Auto-approve Slack post message toggle is back in Local MCP settings — default on, you can opt out. Fresh installs and existing UserDefaults that never had the key set are seeded to on; manual offs persist across launches.
  • Low / Medium / ReadSensitive MCP write prompts now surface via a sheet instead of only the popover chip. Previously, if the popover was closed when a cb_slack_post_message arrived, the prompt was invisible and the call timed out with user_cancelled: gate timed out. (PR #14)
  • New cb_gsheets_create_from_csv — create and populate a Google Sheet from CSV text in one call (RFC 4180 quoting + ragged rows). (PR #15)
  • Google OAuth scope bumped to include drive.file for per-file sharing. Existing tokens will see cb_gsheets_* and cb_gdrive_share_file fail with an insufficient-permission error until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector once.
  • Full cb_gsheets_* + cb_gdrive_share_file set now listed in the Local MCP tool catalog.

item.title: Version 11.23 (BETA)
item.description:
• cb_slack_post_message is now always auto-approved — the on/off toggle has been removed from Local MCP settings.
  • Existing users with the toggle previously off are flipped on automatically on first launch after update; the Go MCP process re-reads the policy JSON immediately so the change applies without restart.
  • Other write tools (Slack thread replies, ClickUp, Google, GitHub, GitLab, SSH) continue to require local approval.

item.title: Version 11.22 (BETA)
item.description:
• Maintenance republish to reconcile the signed .app source with the previously shipped v11.21 artifact — no functional code change vs v11.21.
  • Retains the v11.21 fix: MCP write-tool approval gate uses a two-party handshake. The gateway sends hello, the widget replies ready only after its approval UI bridge has decoded the stream, and queued prompts flush after the readiness signal. Closes the cb_slack_post_message failure mode where the MCP server queued a write prompt before the widget could display it.
  • Retains the v11.20 stdio-proxy hardening (decoupled stdin buffering + exponential-backoff redial across socket drops).

item.title: Version 11.21 (BETA)
item.description:
• Fixed cb_slack_post_message timing out when the backend socket was present but the Claude Bar approval UI had not completed its readiness handshake.
  • The gate IPC now uses a two-party hello / ready handshake before queued write prompts flush, while preserving the v11.20 proxy reconnect hardening.

item.title: Version 11.20 (BETA)
item.description:
• Fixed cb_slack_post_message (and any other MCP tool call routed through the local gateway proxy) failing silently when the backend socket churned mid-request.
  • The stdio proxy now buffers stdin into a channel decoupled from the active Unix-domain-socket connection, redials with exponential backoff, and replays the pending line so MCP requests ride out backend restarts and slow first-boot startup.

item.title: Version 11.19 (BETA)
item.description:
• Auto-swap now blocks on any live interactive or background claude session, not just busy/waiting ones — idle-but-open claude no longer gets swapped underneath you mid-session.
  • Threshold trigger arms a 60-second grace window and fires a notification ("Auto-swap in 60s — will switch when no claude session is running"); the actual swap only fires after the deadline AND when the safety gate still holds.
  • Pending swap auto-cancels if the active percent drops back below threshold during the grace window (manual swap, 5-hour window reset).
  • Menu-bar safety badge relabeled from "claude busy" to "claude running" to match the new semantics.

item.title: Version 11.18 (BETA)
item.description:
Hotfix — Keychain prompt no longer fires after Sparkle auto-update.

  • No more macOS Keychain "Allow access?" prompt on launch after a Sparkle auto-update. The iCloud-sync toggle reset used to live inside the popover's lazy .task, so a background update + a user who hadn't yet opened the popover let the backup-token refresh run while iCloudSyncEnabled still held the previous version's true value. The new code signature hit the saved passphrase Keychain item's ACL and macOS surfaced its password prompt. Reset now runs in App.init() right after the settings migration, before anything touches the Keychain.

item.title: Version 11.17 (BETA)
item.description:
Hotfix for v11.16 popover regression.

  • Hotfix for v11.16. Yesterday's deactivate-dismissal fix used hidesOnDeactivate = true on the popover NSWindow. AppKit hid the window the first time the user clicked into another app, but SwiftUI MenuBarExtra's internal "popover is shown" flag stayed true — so the next menu-bar click no-op'd and the popover never reopened. Only recovery was to quit and relaunch Claude Bar.
  • Same dismissal behaviour, fixed plumbing. A NotificationCenter observer on NSApplication.didResignActiveNotification now routes the close through MenuBarPopoverToggle.closeIfOpen() — the same performClick path SwiftUI uses internally, so the toggle state stays in sync. Click into another app → popover dismisses; click the menu-bar icon → popover re-renders normally.

item.title: Version 11.16 (BETA)
item.description:
Full popover polish + popover dismissal fixes.

  • Full popover layout polish. Threshold slider track + accent fill thinned by 1pt, the "you are HERE" tick recoloured from orange to black and narrowed by 1pt, and the Auto-swap + Token-usage section now lines up with the 14pt horizontal inset of the accounts header above.
  • Menu-bar popover dismisses on app deactivation. Click into Chrome / Slack / Finder / Terminal and the popover hides automatically — matches Terminal.app and Cloudflare WARP. Settings / Daily / Briefing inside Claude Bar continue to layer correctly.
  • Rename sheet no longer collapses the popover. SwiftUI's MenuBarExtra dismisses the popover the instant another window becomes key; we now re-open it on the next runloop tick so the account row stays visible behind the Rename dialog.
  • MCP Confirm overlay renders as a solid card. Background is now .regularMaterial with a slim risk-tint border, and long Slack / ClickUp arg payloads truncate from the middle instead of pushing the layout out of the popover frame. Previously the popover's slider / KPI cards / chart labels bled through the overlay and collided visually with the gate copy.

item.title: Version 11.15 (BETA)
item.description:
One-click Google OAuth reconnect — closes #16.

  • One-click Google OAuth reconnect. The Google Drive connector now exposes a dedicated Connect OAuth… / Reconnect OAuth… button that wipes the cached grant in Keychain and immediately re-runs the Connect Google flow, minting a fresh token with every scope the current build registers (drive, drive.file, spreadsheets, calendar.readonly, gmail.readonly). The v11.14 workaround of "Disconnect, then Connect" is no longer needed.
  • Fixed cb_gsheets_* tools returning ACCESS_TOKEN_SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT for anyone who installed before v11.14. Their OAuth grant predates the spreadsheets + drive.file scopes; one tap of Reconnect OAuth… fixes it.

item.title: Version 11.14 (BETA)
item.description:
Reliable MCP write-tool gate surfacing + new Drive share tool.

  • MCP write-tool approval prompts now reliably surface within seconds of the LLM call — Slack post, Google Sheets write, GitHub PR comment, ClickUp updates, and every other gated MCP tool open the gate popover above any Settings / Daily panel and fire a banner notification. Previously gated calls had been silently timing out since the gate IPC was introduced in v11.4 because of two stacked bugs (GateCoordinator getting ARC-released before prompts arrived, and the JSON envelope's RFC3339 timestamp failing to decode); both are fixed here.
  • Added cb_gdrive_share_file — grant reader / commenter / writer access to one Drive file via the gated MCP write path. Existing v11.x Google connectors need a Disconnect + Reconnect (Settings → MCP) to mint a fresh token with the new drive.file scope before this tool can run; Drive list / read tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
  • Structured gate-IPC logging — ~/Library/Logs/ClaudeBar/claude-bar.log now reports every hello / prompt / decode-failure / notification result with the nonce prefix, so future IPC schema drift or permission regressions surface immediately instead of dropping prompts silently.

item.title: Version 11.13 (BETA)
item.description:
MCP write-approval surfacing fix.

  • MCP write-tool approval prompts now reliably surface even when Focus / Do Not Disturb is on or another Claude Bar panel is open. The gate banner uses .timeSensitive interruption so DND no longer suppresses it within the 60-second approval window, and the auto-opened popover lifts above any Settings / Daily window that might otherwise have hidden the approve / cancel buttons. Previously gated calls (Slack post, Sheets write, GitHub PR comment, etc.) could time out invisibly — Claude Code would report user_cancelled: gate timed out with no on-screen indication.

item.title: Version 11.12 (BETA)
item.description:
UI hotfix.

  • Fixed the stale "Couldn't fetch usage" warning persisting on web-linked rows whose scrape was actually succeeding. A single transient OAuth probe failure used to leave the orange warning chip on the row forever — even after the next web scrape returned valid 5h/7d values. The error field now clears whenever fresh usage data lands.

item.title: Version 11.11 (BETA)
item.description:
Web-usage scraper unstuck + popover anti-hang + Full-layout token-chart toggle.

  • Web-linked accounts update again. Three scrape bugs combined to freeze usage at stale values: the labelling-cell walk aborted at the progressbar's empty parent; the 7d classifier required the word "Weekly" inside the bar cell (it lives in the heading above on Max plans); and a hung WKWebView reload could block the refresh cycle for minutes. All three fixed; added a hard 12-second scrape timeout.
  • Popover anti-hang. Refresh calls now coalesce — manual "Refresh web usage" clicks, the timer loop, post-swap re-refreshes, and the launch refresh can no longer stack on top of each other and saturate the main actor.
  • Optional Token-usage chart on Full layout. Settings → General → Popover layout has a new "Show token usage chart in Full layout" toggle (default off). When off, the bottom token chart + KPI cards collapse and the popover shrinks by ~220pt.
  • Settings stops floating above Chrome. Reverted Settings from popUpMenu + 1 to normal level — other apps can cover it like any standard panel. The popover briefly lifts above Settings only while you preview a layout change.
  • OAuth fallback error isolation. A failing terminal account (expired creds / 429) no longer aborts the entire refresh cycle and freezes every account at "Loading…".

item.title: Version 11.10 (BETA)
item.description:
Hotfix — back off the v11.9 web-poll cadence.

  • Web-linked refresh cadence backed off from 60s / 30s to 120s / 60s. The 60s cycle was too tight for multi-account setups — each web scrape costs 3–8s (hidden WKWebView reload + React SPA hydrate) plus a keychain/iCloud cookie restore+save, so at 60s × N accounts the main actor saturated and the popover stopped updating.
  • Still ~33% faster than the OAuth default (180s / 120s) for web-linked accounts; OAuth /usage cadence and its independent gating are unchanged.

item.title: Version 11.9 (BETA)
item.description:
Faster refresh cycle for web-linked accounts.

  • Web-linked accounts now refresh every 60 seconds (every 30 seconds when the active account's 5h usage is ≥ 80%), instead of waiting the OAuth-pinned 180 / 120 second cycle. The web scrape rides your own claude.ai cookie session via WKWebView, so it doesn't share Anthropic's rate-limited /usage budget — usage bars and auto-swap react ~3× faster for web-linked accounts.
  • OAuth /usage cadence (180s / 120s, user-configurable via refreshIntervalSec / refreshIntervalHighSec) is preserved for terminal-only accounts and is independently gated, so the faster outer loop doesn't burn the rate-limited endpoint.

item.title: Version 11.8 (BETA)
item.description:
Right-click menu everywhere + accurate web-usage percentages.

  • Right-click any account in the Standard or Tiny popover for the full action menu — Connect / Open / Refresh / Disconnect web usage, Rename, Switch, Force switch, Remove. Previously only reachable from the Full layout's row context menu and the ellipsis button.
  • Web-linked accounts now report the same percentage shown on claude.ai. The scraper used to read a raw aria-valuenow value that did not match the displayed "X% used" text (we observed 14% for a bar the page rendered as 7%, and 52% for 59%); it now parses the visible percentage from the bar's labelling cell and only falls back to a normalised aria-valuenow / aria-valuemax reading if no text label is present.

item.title: Version 11.7 (BETA)
item.description:
Layout fix for the Full popover.

  • Full popover stops clipping the third account row. With three accounts where two of them showed a "Needs login" or usage-error chip, the in-row badge added ~17pt that the popover hadn't budgeted for — the third row's 5h / 7d bars hid under the Auto-swap section because the bounded ScrollView clipped them and scroll was disabled at three accounts. The popover frame now sums each visible row's actual height (base + badge extras), so the third row stays fully visible regardless of credential state.

item.title: Version 11.6 (BETA)
item.description:
Follow-up safety net for #6.

  • Usage scrape bails on ambiguous DOM wrappers. v11.5 anchored each progressbar to its smallest labelling ancestor and dropped per-model weekly bars; this release adds the safety net that was missing for a degenerate shape — when one wrapper carries both the "current session" label and the "weekly" label (so the scraper can't tell which window the progressbar belongs to), the slot is now surfaced as Unavailable instead of guessed.

item.title: Version 11.5 (BETA)
item.description:
Closes #7 and #6 — rename works first try and usage windows read the correct numbers.

  • Rename profile now works on the first Save. Three-dot menu → Rename… used to race the popover dismissal: first Save closed both popovers and skipped the rename, only the retry stuck. The rename form lives in a dedicated floating window now (same pattern as Add account) so its state is fully decoupled from the popover lifecycle.
  • Web usage scrape now reports each window correctly. Anthropic added per-model weekly bars (Weekly · Opus / Sonnet / Haiku) alongside the all-models bar — accounts with Opus pinned at 100% were rendering 7d as 100% with headroom left, and certain compact layouts collapsed 5h and 7d to identical numbers. Scraping now anchors on each progressbar, classifies its smallest labelling ancestor, and drops per-model weekly blocks so the headline limit is what you see.

item.title: Version 11.4 (BETA)
item.description:
Closes #11 — MCP approval prompts no longer time out silently.

  • MCP approval prompts now actually surface. Gated write tools (cb_slack_post_message, cb_gsheets_*, cb_github_create_pr, …) used to time out silently when the popover was closed (which it always is while you're in Claude Code). The widget now auto-opens the popover and fires a system notification with the tool + summary on every new prompt, so the confirm UI is one click away regardless of focus.
  • Approval window widened from 30s to 60s. Backend gate timeout and widget countdown bar share a single constant so they stay in lock-step.
  • Gate IPC proxy starts at launch. Previously spawned lazily on first popover render, so MCP writes before you'd ever opened the menu came back with "no emitter configured". Now starts unconditionally from applicationDidFinishLaunching.

item.title: Version 11.3 (BETA)
item.description:
Closes #10 — Google Sheets write tools.

  • Google Sheets write tools. Three new tools — cb_gsheets_create_spreadsheet, cb_gsheets_update_values, cb_gsheets_append_values — turn an ad-hoc data structure into a real spreadsheet with addressable cells. Google OAuth scope bumps to include spreadsheets; existing users must Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector once to upgrade the token.
  • Disconnect now keeps your saved credentials. Disconnect leaves the Keychain payload intact; Reconnect verifies the saved token against the provider and re-enables without re-paste / re-OAuth. Works for Slack, ClickUp, GitHub, GDrive (GitLab + Bitwarden go straight to the add sheet).
  • Replace + Forget. Replace… rotates a token while a connector is still enabled. Forget hard-deletes the Keychain payload AND the registry entry for security rotation.

item.title: Version 11.2 (BETA)
item.description:
Fixes #2 — toggling MCP connectors in Settings used to silently no-op.

  • Fixed the MCP connector toggle in Settings → Local MCP. Turning a connector OFF now actually disables its tools. Verified end-to-end across all five connectors (Slack, ClickUp, Google, GitHub, GitLab): OFF drops the service's tools from tools/list on the next message; ON restores them.
  • Shared connector toggle is now authoritative. A stale per-account Enabled=true flag left over from an older iCloud restore can no longer keep a service in tools/list after the shared toggle is flipped off.
  • Registry auto-prunes stale per-account Enabled flags at load time so debug dumps and cloud bundle pushes reflect the same state the UI shows.

item.title: Version 11.1 (BETA)
item.description:
• iCloud Sync now keeps Claude Code OAuth credentials local. Account roster, MCP connector tokens (Slack / ClickUp / Google / GitLab), and claude.ai web cookies still roam between Macs — but each new Mac runs its own claude /login per account. A stolen iCloud bundle can never authenticate as you.
  • Multi-Mac merge is additive. Connecting Slack on Mac A and ClickUp on Mac B no longer drops one of the tokens when their pushes interleave; both connectors survive. Shared connectors merge the same way.
  • Removed the Daily Briefing feature entirely. Settings → Briefing tab, the standalone Briefing window, Daily Plan / Command modes, the news-feed scheduler, and the Carbon ⌥X hotkey are gone. Daily window is chat-only now.

item.title: Version 11
item.description:
• Settings window redesigned. Sidebar now groups tabs under App, Features, Data & Sync, and System. Accounts, IDE Integration, iCloud Sync, and Diagnostics are first-class tabs instead of being buried inside General or Update.
  • Sidebar search (⌘F). Type to filter tabs by name; Enter picks the first match, Esc clears. Each tab also gets its own tinted icon so the sidebar is scannable at a glance.
  • About panel hero — app icon, version, channel badge, and a direct "Check for updates…" button without leaving the page.
  • Empty-state hero on the Accounts tab when no accounts are enrolled, so the first-launch path leads with a single big CTA instead of an empty list.
  • Settings remembers the last tab you were viewing between opens.
  • Fixed the 7-day usage bar flickering on and off for web-linked accounts. The claude.ai scraper now waits for both the 5h and 7d blocks to hydrate before returning.
  • Fixed the brief layout shift when opening Settings → Local MCP the first time after launch — the MCP coordinator is now pre-warmed at startup.

item.title: Version 10.49 (BETA)
item.description:
• Slimmed the Medium popover. The boxed "Token usage" card was replaced by the Tiny-style Day / Week / Month strip, and the row + shell heights were retuned to remove ~65pt of dead chrome. Both layouts now read identically when switching between them.
  • MCP tool-cost catalog now includes GitLab. Measurement previously ran with a nil GitLab instance store, so every GitLab tool row showed 0; the gateway is now wired with throw-away stub stores so the connector's tools contribute their measured schema-byte cost to the catalog.

item.title: Version 10.48 (BETA)
item.description:
• Adding a GitLab self-host instance now gives proper feedback. The form shows "Adding…" with a spinner during the round-trip, then either an inline green "Successfully added 'Name'" confirmation that auto-closes after 1.2 seconds, or an inline red error so you can fix the field and retry without closing the window.
  • Settings → MCP connectors list now reflects GitLab as connected once an instance is added. Previously the row stayed stuck at "not connected" forever because GitLab instance secrets live in a separate per-instance Keychain slot the row didn't read; the backend now mirrors the connection state into the shared connector flag the row does read.
  • Removing the last GitLab instance flips the connector back to "not connected" automatically.

item.title: Version 10.47 (BETA)
item.description:
• Tiny popover height now hugs its content. The whitespace strip that used to sit between the last account row and the Day / Week / Month token strip is gone — account list is bounded with an exact row-count height, rowHeight tightened to 38pt, inner paddings on the token strip and auto-swap bar trimmed.
  • Tiny popover scroll cap is now 4 rows. Past 4 accounts the list scrolls inside its bounded frame; the token strip and auto-swap controls stay anchored at the bottom.
  • Empty-account state in Tiny uses a fixed 90pt slot instead of collapsing arbitrarily.

item.title: Version 10.46 (BETA)
item.description:
• Full popover layout restructured to match Standard. Auto-swap slider and the token-usage chart are pinned to the bottom regardless of account count; up to three accounts the popover doesn't scroll at all, four or more accounts only scroll the account list. Frame shrank from 440×760 down to 400×716, and a hairline border was added so the popover edge stays crisp on bright wallpapers.
  • Tiny popover gained a Day / Week / Month token-usage strip in the previously-empty space below the account list — same data source as Full's chart so numbers stay in lock-step.
  • Tiny popover threshold slider no longer snaps back when you grab the knob. Both the parent and knob drag gestures now report the cursor position in the same named track coordinate space.
  • Tiny popover account chips align cleanly down the right edge regardless of digit count — the 5h label slot and the value slot are pinned to fixed widths so "0%" rows no longer slide left relative to "10%" rows.
  • Settings window gets the pointing-hand cursor on every Button, Toggle, Picker, Stepper, and Link via a single AppKit cursor tracker installed on the Settings NSWindow — no per-Button decoration needed.

item.title: Version 10.45 (BETA)
item.description:
• Settings → MCP redesigned in Apple style. Connector rows become rounded cards with an SF Symbol, name, and small tinted status pill on top; Tools / Prompt are subtle chevron links instead of bright pills; the tool list drops the zebra stripes for native macOS Settings sectioning — small grey category captions, rounded cards, hairline dividers, and a summary chip "N of M tools enabled · X tokens / message" at the top of the expanded list. Priority badges (Essential, Advanced) are low-saturation tinted capsules.
  • Standard popover only scrolls the account list now. Auto-swap and Token-usage cards are pinned to the bottom regardless of how many accounts you have. Up to three accounts no scroll at all; four or more scroll inside the account list only.
  • Tiny popover's threshold slider no longer twitches on digit-count boundaries during drag — current and trigger render into fixed-width slots so the row stays still.
  • Tiny popover width restored to 290pt — the 227pt squeeze in v10.44 broke the 5h/7d chip text onto multiple lines.
  • Menu-bar popover now floats above the Settings window so picking a layout in Settings → General actually shows the live preview instead of hiding it behind the panel.

item.title: Version 10.44 (BETA)
item.description:
• Settings → MCP tool list is now a table with a TOKENS column. Every tool's cost in Claude Code's system prompt is measured live — the backend builds tools/list once, serialises each entry, and reports JSON-bytes ÷ 4. Cost numbers tint green / amber / red for light / medium / heavy tools, and a floating chip in the header shows the running total currently enabled for the connector. Tool ID surfaces underneath the description for grep / docs hunting.
  • Tiny popover layout slimmed to ~2/3 of its previous width (340 → 227pt). The 5h/7d chips still read at 13pt monospaced; only long account names truncate earlier.

item.title: Version 10.43 (BETA)
item.description:
• Per-tool enable/disable for every MCP connector. Open Settings → MCP, click the new purple Tools (k/n) pill on any connector row to expand a categorised list — Essential tools at the top (green badge), Common in the middle, Advanced (writes, destructive) at the bottom (grey badge). Each row carries a one-line description and an on/off toggle. Disabled tools are stripped from tools/list entirely, dropping their schema bytes from Claude Code's system prompt and blocking calls. Toggling one auto-restarts running claude sessions so the change takes effect immediately. Catalog covers all 85 currently-shipped MCP tools across Slack, ClickUp, Google (Drive / Gmail / Calendar), GitHub, GitLab, and Bitwarden.
  • Settings → General → Popover layout now auto-opens the menu-bar popover when you pick a layout, so you can preview Full / Standard / Tiny side-by-side with the settings window and decide without committing.

item.title: Version 10.42 (BETA)
item.description:
• Standard and Tiny popover layouts now ship full auto-swap controls. Standard's auto-swap card swaps the read-only threshold display for a live toggle + draggable slider with the same traffic-light current marker as Full. Tiny gains a compact auto-swap strip at the bottom — toggle + slider + status (on / off / paused) — so the smallest layout can still drive auto-swap without opening Settings.
  • Settings → Update tab now hosts the legacy Diagnostics groups (iCloud sync wizard, schema verify, credential refresh, web-usage check, logs) at the bottom under an Advanced divider — moved from the retired Diagnostics tab so nothing was lost in the consolidation.

item.title: Version 10.41 (BETA)
item.description:
• New Settings → Update tab consolidates everything related to keeping the app current. The Auto-update toggle (moved from General → Updates) plus Check for updates… button + release notes + version info (moved from About) now all live in one focused panel, with the installed-build version, channel badge and build date right above the changelog.
  • About tab trims down to a one-line breadcrumb pointing at the new Update tab, plus the static Author / Tech Stack / Welcome flow / Legal sections.

item.title: Version 10.40 (BETA)
item.description: • Every 5h / 7d usage percentage now renders in a single, consistent blue across the Full, Standard, and Tiny popover layouts. The numeric value previously shifted hue per quota tier (green / amber / red), which made low and high values look like different fields — now the digit weight is constant and only the bar fill / chip background carries the traffic-light tier signal.

item.title: Version 10.39 (BETA)
item.description:
• Standard popover — each account now shows 5h and 7d usage as a labelled bar with a readable percentage in monospaced digits, tinted with the same green / amber / red traffic-light palette. The old pair of 5pt-tall bars was a width-only encoding nobody could read at a glance.
  • Tiny popover — 5h / 7d chips bumped to 13pt semibold (vs old 10pt) with bolder borders, legible even with the popover off to one side of the screen.
  • Trimmed wasted shell padding in both Standard and Tiny — about 70pt less vertical empty space in Standard, no more bottom strip below the last row in Tiny.

item.title: Version 10.38 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover layout picker now offers three sizes — Full, Standard, Tiny — in Settings → General → Popover layout.
    • Full — original dashboard (status header, usage bars, auto-swap slider, token chart).
    • Standard — 2/3-scaled compact dashboard with twin 5h/7d bars and small auto-swap + token KPI cards.
    • Tiny — header + account list only; each row carries avatar, name and tiny colour-coded 5h X% / 7d Y% chips.
  • Switching takes effect immediately, no restart.
  • Users on the previous "Minimum" layout are migrated automatically to Standard so nothing changes underfoot.

item.title: Version 10.37 (BETA)
item.description:
• New "Auto-update Claude Bar" toggle in Settings → General → Updates. When on, Claude Bar polls the appcast daily, silently downloads any new EdDSA-signed build, and installs it on the next idle moment — no prompt, no manual relaunch. Default ON; turn off to keep manual control via About → Check for updates….
  • New "Popover layout" picker in Settings → General. Switch between Standard (full status, accounts with usage bars, auto-swap slider, token chart) and Minimum (header + account list only, click-to-swap). Toggling takes effect immediately, no restart.

item.title: Version 10.36 (BETA)
item.description:
• ACTIVE chip is now green across every theme. Rainbow used to render it purple/magenta which read like decoration; one colour everywhere makes "this is the live account" unmissable.
  • Current-usage marker on the auto-swap slider is now bright orange and 4× thicker. No more squinting at a 2-pixel hairline to find where you are versus the trigger.
  • The current X% legend below the slider now uses the same green/amber/red traffic-light palette as the per-account usage bars, so the slider visually agrees with the rest of the popover.

item.title: Version 10.35 (BETA)
item.description:
• Disabling a Local MCP connector now actually saves context tokens. Previously the on/off switch only blocked tool calls — schemas still shipped in every system prompt. Now disabled connectors are skipped at MCP server startup, so their schemas never reach tools/list. Expect ~1.5k–7.5k fewer tokens per message depending on which connectors you turn off.
  • Toggling a connector auto-restarts your running `claude` CLI sessions (SIGINT → claude-watch resumes) so the new toolset takes effect immediately. Account credentials are not touched. cmux panes are skipped to avoid racing the cmux relauncher.
  • GitHub tool schemas slimmed ~38% — descriptions on self-explanatory fields (owner, repo, number) dropped, enum hints compressed. Saves ~1,900 tokens per message whenever GitHub is enabled, with no change in tool behavior.

item.title: Version 10.34 (BETA)
item.description:
• Per-connector on/off toggle in Local MCP. Slack, ClickUp, GitHub, Google, and GitLab each get a switch — silence a provider's tools without deleting the token from your Keychain. Re-enable instantly when you need it back.
  • Local MCP tab reordered: connectors sit at the top, with Tool permissions and Gateway moved below so the controls you touch most are right under your cursor.

item.title: Version 10.33 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover height now adapts to the number of accounts — 1 account is compact, 2 is mid, 3+ is full height with the list scrolling internally. Smoothly resizes when you add or remove an account.
  • Fixed ⌥X (Daily Briefing) firing on the wrong BriefingCoordinator instance. The hotkey now routes through a shared singleton so it always hits the live SwiftUI-owned coordinator.

item.title: Version 10.32 (BETA)
item.description:
• Header is now slim: status on the left, three evenly-spaced buttons on the right (Theme · Quit · Settings). Settings anchors the top-right corner.
  • Add account moved into Settings → General → "Accounts".
  • Verify all + Force refresh moved into Settings → Diagnostics, with inline outcome / cooldown state.
  • Briefing pill is gone — use ⌥X for Daily Briefing and ⌥Z to toggle the popover, same as before.

item.title: Version 10.31 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover now scrolls when content runs long. Header status no longer gets clipped at the top, Token-usage KPI strip no longer cut off at the bottom on dense screens.
  • GitLab MCP add sheet redrawn — fixed-width label column, longer fields, larger window — so labels like "Personal Access Token" don't clip into the edge.
  • Fixed "Check for updates…" rendering on the popover instead of on Settings when invoked from About. The update overlay is mounted in both places now, so it lands centered on whichever window you're using.

item.title: Version 10.30 (BETA)
item.description:
• Alerts and floating sheets triggered from Settings now sit on top of the Settings window and centered on it — previously they could land behind Settings or off to one side.
  • Fixed /exit in claude-watch feeling like a freeze. Post-exit credential-change window shrank 8s → 2s, and the background session watcher is now SIGKILL'd on cleanup so the shell prompt actually returns.

item.title: Version 10.29 (BETA)
item.description:
• Footer is gone. Every global action (Add account, Verify all, Force refresh, Health check, Theme, Quit, Settings) lives in the header now. Settings anchors the top-right corner; Verify all is icon-only.
  • MCP tab drops the per-account override rows. Only the flat shared connectors list shows — no more six duplicate "using shared" entries below the canonical row.
  • Web-linked accounts now use WebView usage as source of truth. OAuth (the terminal-side /api/oauth/usage path) only fires when the web scrape returned absolutely nothing for that cycle.
  • Auto-swap threshold bar is taller, easier to grab, and stays a single accent blue instead of shifting through yellow/orange as you drag.
  • Fixed claude-watch refusing to let /exit when you had manually swapped accounts mid-session — the watcher now snapshots credentials at the moment claude exits and only auto-restarts on a real post-exit swap.

item.title: Version 10.28 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover shrank to 440×720. Only what you actually need to see: header, accounts, auto-swap, footer.
  • Settings now opens as a real centered window with a left sidebar (App / System groups). It survives the popover collapsing and only closes when you actually close it.
  • MCP tab → GitLab → Connect now opens the multi-instance Add sheet directly, instead of falling back to the generic single-token paste flow.
  • Fixed ⌥Z and ⌥X hotkeys not firing on a cold-launched session — registration now happens at app launch instead of waiting for the popover's first open.

item.title: Version 10.27 (BETA)
item.description:
• MCP Cancel now actually dismisses the connect window. When the menu-bar popover collapses behind the floating sheet, Cancel used to clear coordinator state but leave the window on screen. The sheet now closes the window directly.
  • Briefing pill pinned to true center. No more drift when the status text on the left grows or shrinks.

item.title: Version 10.26 (BETA)
item.description: • Briefing entry moved to the header. The Daily Briefing button now sits as a centered pill in the popover header instead of in the footer — fewer footer icons, more prominent placement.

item.title: Version 10.25 (BETA)
item.description:
• Swap no longer asks you to re-login on a transient OAuth blip. Rate-limit (429) and network errors during refresh now defer the swap with a clear retry hint instead of telling you to re-authenticate.
  • Polished failure card. When a swap can't complete, you get an elegant overlay with icon, plain-language explanation, and a one-click Retry or Re-login button.
  • Backup credential safety. Keychain ACL migration retries its re-add step, so a transient Keychain hiccup can no longer orphan a backup blob.
  • ~/.claude.json read race fix. Swap absorbs the partial-write race when Claude Code rewrites the config concurrently.
  • 30s swap timeout. No more hanging on a stale lock — fails fast with an actionable message.
  • Footer drops the Briefing button (Daily Briefing still opens via ⌥X).

item.title: Version 10.24 (BETA)
item.description: • Removed the empty space below the Today / This week / This month strip. The token-usage chart now grows to fill the popover so the KPI strip sits flush above the footer regardless of how many accounts you have.

item.title: Version 10.23 (BETA)
item.description: • Settings now uses a top tab strip instead of the left sidebar. General, MCP, Briefing, Privacy, Diagnostics, and About sit as inline chips across the top of the Settings overlay, giving each detail pane the full modal width.

item.title: Version 10.22 (BETA)
item.description:
• Popover redesign — no more top tab strip. Opening Claude Bar lands you straight on a single scrollable surface: account list (3 visible, scroll past), Auto-swap controls, then the token-usage chart with Today / Week / Month totals. No more bouncing between Dashboard, Accounts, Stats, and Settings tabs to find anything.
  • Footer adds Add account and Settings buttons. Add account opens the bilingual EN/VI guidance card with an "Open Terminal & run claude /login" action; Settings opens the sidebar (General, MCP, Briefing, Privacy, Diagnostics, About) as a modal overlay dismissible with Esc or by clicking outside.
  • Account list scrolls past 3 accounts (was 6) so the chart stays visible even with several accounts added.
  • "Verify all", per-row Switch, and the ⋯ context menu keep their existing behaviour.

item.title: Version 10.21 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix — second macOS Keychain prompt on update is gone. v10.19 blocked the iCloud-sync passphrase prompt; this build also blocks the claude.ai web profile prompt (one Keychain item per linked account email). Combined, every Sparkle update now lands with zero ACL prompts unless you flip the toggle on yourself.
  • What's new. The Diagnostics "Enable iCloud sync" toggle now also gates the web profile Keychain cache. Same toggle, broader scope: when off, Claude Bar never reads any Keychain item.
  • Known issue. When the toggle is off, claude.ai web profiles still work (cookies live in WKWebsiteDataStore on disk) but won't be restored from the Keychain cache. If a web profile fails to load after upgrade, flip the toggle on once and restart polling.

item.title: Version 10.20 (BETA)
item.description:
• Menu-bar popover collapsed from 8 tabs to 4. The old layout crammed Accounts, General, MCP, Claude, Daily, Diagnostics, Privacy, and About across the top — with the "Claude" tab mixing the account list, the auto-swap toggle, and the token-usage chart on one screen. v10.20 collapses those eight into Dashboard, Accounts, Stats, and Settings.
  • New Dashboard is now the default landing tab. Active account at a glance with a one-click switch dropdown, plus Today / Week / Month token totals.
  • Auto-swap controls moved into the Accounts tab — next to the list they actually affect, instead of buried under "Claude".
  • Settings is one home for everything configurable: General, MCP, Briefing, Privacy, Diagnostics, and About all live behind a single sidebar.
  • Footer "Daily" button renamed to "Briefing" so it no longer collides visually with the Settings sub-tab of the same name.

item.title: Version 10.19 (BETA)
item.description:
• What's new — iCloud sync toggle now resets to OFF on every Sparkle update. Each new build lands on a clean default-off, so the macOS Keychain prompt only ever appears when you explicitly opt in to sync for that release. Within the same version the toggle keeps your choice across launches; it only resets when the version string changes.
  • Hotfix. Replaced the v10.18 one-shot force-off with a per-version reset, so the default-off promise holds for every future update — not just the upgrade from v10.16.
  • Known issue. If you sync across Macs, re-enable the toggle in Diagnostics → iCloud Sync once after every update. Your bundle and passphrase remain intact in iCloud between updates.

item.title: Version 10.18 (BETA)
item.description:
• Hotfix — iCloud sync toggle now actually defaults off. v10.17 documented default-off, but a stale UserDefaults value written by v10.16's auto-migration kept the toggle stuck on for upgraders. This build runs a one-shot reset so the toggle reads off after upgrading, matching the documented behavior.
  • Known issue. If you were syncing on v10.16 or earlier, re-enable the toggle once in Diagnostics → iCloud Sync to resume cross-Mac sync. Your bundle and passphrase remain intact in iCloud.

item.title: Version 10.17 (BETA)
item.description:
• What's new — About panel now shows per-release notes inline. Open About → Claude Bar to read what's new, hotfixes, and known issues for the version you're running. The channel badge follows the version: bare-integer = Stable (green), dotted = Beta (orange).
  • iCloud sync defaults to OFF for everyone. The v10.16 migration that silently auto-enabled sync for users with an existing bundle is gone — the toggle in Diagnostics → iCloud Sync is now off after upgrade, even for prior sync users. Flip it back on to resume cross-Mac sync.
  • Hotfix — Command Center loads cleanly with no SSH hosts / GitLab instances. Empty lists no longer surface a red "decodingFailed: found null" error in the Diagnostics tab.
  • Known issue. Existing iCloud sync users will see the toggle off after this update — your bundle and passphrase are untouched in iCloud, just re-enable the toggle to resume.

item.title: Version 10.16 (BETA)
item.description:
• BETA RELEASE — not stable. Wide change surface this cycle (20 new MCP tools across 6 services + a Keychain-ACL prompt fix that touches launch-time code paths). If you depend on Claude Bar for daily account-switch work, stay on v10.15 until the next stable spin.
  • Updates no longer trigger a macOS "Allow ClaudeBar to access this keychain item?" prompt every time you upgrade. iCloud sync is now an explicit toggle in Diagnostics (off by default). Users who were already syncing get auto-migrated on first launch — your existing bundle and passphrase survive. Turning the toggle off doesn't delete anything; it just stops Claude Bar reading the Keychain until you opt back in.
  • GitLab MCP grew from 7 to 19 tools, finally reaching parity with the GitHub surface. New reads: MR diff, MR / issue notes, MR changes, get_issue, get_file (raw text at a ref), list_branches, list_commits, list_pipelines. New gated writes: create_issue, create_mr (draft-aware), update_issue.
  • Google Workspace MCP filled out: Drive gained list_folder + download_file (raw bytes for non-Doc files), Calendar gained list_calendars + get_free_busy, Gmail gained list_labels + get_thread (full conversation with every message expanded).
  • SSH MCP added read_file — head -c with a server-side byte cap (default 100 KiB, max 5 MiB). Safer than exec("cat") when the agent just needs a config file's contents.
  • Bitwarden MCP added list_folders so the agent can discover folder IDs before constraining a vault search, instead of asking you for them.

item.title: Version 10.15
item.description:
• Connect window now shows a green "Successfully connected" confirmation for 1.2 seconds before auto-closing, so you can see the MCP actually wired up instead of the panel just vanishing. Applies to every connector — Slack, ClickUp, GitHub, and Google.
  • GitHub MCP grew from 9 to 28 tools. Reads now cover the missing single-gets (issue, commit, file content at a ref), PR review threads and inline comments, files / commits in a PR, branches, commit history, issue search, CI check-runs and Actions workflow runs. Writes add create_issue, create_pr (draft-aware), update_issue (title/body/labels/assignees/milestone), request_reviewers, add_labels, and remove_label — all surface a confirm gate before hitting GitHub.
  • Slack MCP went from 3 to 9 tools. New reads: channel history (so the agent can see what is happening in a channel right now, not just search hits), user list, single user lookup, and message permalink. New gated writes: post_message and reply_thread.
  • ClickUp MCP added 4 tools. Reads: list_comments (closes the gap where the agent could not read comment bodies), list_members (resolve assignee IDs to names), and search_tasks (cross-workspace filter without iterating list-by-list). Write: update_task to edit name / description / priority / due / start, alongside the existing update_task_status.

item.title: Version 10.14
item.description:
• GitHub MCP connector now supports a personal access token (PAT). Open Settings → Local MCP, click Connect on the GitHub row, paste a token from github.com/settings/tokens — no OAuth app setup required. Both classic (ghp_…) and fine-grained (github_pat_…) tokens are accepted.
  • Fixed alerts and file pickers (rename account, cloud passphrase, avatar pick, repo link, chat attachment, diagnostics export/import, swap confirm) appearing behind the menu-bar popover or jumping to a different Space.

item.title: Version 10.13
item.description:
• Connect failures in the MCP connector window now surface inline with a red error banner and progress indicator, instead of silently doing nothing while the popover hid the result.
  • Added orange format hints when the pasted token / client ID does not match the expected prefix (Slack xoxp-/xoxe-/xoxb-, ClickUp pk_, Google .apps.googleusercontent.com) so typos are caught before the round-trip.

item.title: Version 10.12
item.description:
• MCP connector inputs (Slack, ClickUp, Google) no longer collapse the menu-bar popover when you click into a field.
  • Silenced automatic "new version available" notifications — check manually from About when you want them.

item.title: Version 10.11
item.description:
• Auto-resume Claude inside cmux terminal panes after each account swap so the conversation continues under the new account — no toggle required.
  • Panes pinning an isolated CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (for example ~/.codex-accounts/claude/<id>/) are intentionally skipped; claude-bar's global swap does not reach them.

item.title: Version 10.10
item.description:
• Fixed cross-device Claude web cookie sync so two Macs no longer overwrite each other's fresh session and silently log you out.
  • Restore now picks the most recent cookies between local Keychain and the iCloud bundle instead of always trusting local.
  • Expired cookies are filtered on save and restore, and SameSite is preserved across sync round-trips.

item.title: Version 10.9
item.description:
• New command-center UI with session panel, capture box, servers rail, scheduler settings, and notifications.
  • MCP integrations for GitLab, ClickUp capture, and Bitwarden, plus write tools and audit hardening.
  • SSH server manager with ControlMaster, age-encrypted bundles, and tail/exec from MCP.
  • Destructive action gate with widget UI and UDS IPC bridge.
  • Popover destructive-gate sheet now dismisses correctly after MCP connect.

item.title: Version 10.8
item.description: • 'Check for updates…' no longer dismisses the popover. The whole update flow — checking, update found, download, install — now renders as an overlay centred on the popover, and the popover only closes on the usual outside-click.

item.title: Version 10.7
item.description:
• Daily window opens and closes faster — animation tightened so the resize no longer feels like it lags behind the click.
  • Daily and the menu-bar popover now auto-dismiss the moment you switch to another app (Chrome, Xcode, etc.), so they stop covering your real workspace.

item.title: Version 10.6
item.description: • Fixed popover opening on the wrong display in extended/duplicate setups — it now follows the cursor's screen instead of always sticking to the menu-bar status item's screen.

item.title: Version 10.5
item.description: • Fixed Daily window getting orphaned and unclosable after rapid ⌥X hotkey toggles — the X button and hotkey now reliably close it.

item.title: Version 10.4
item.description:
• Welcome onboarding now scrolls and surfaces the bilingual add-account guidance card up front, so first-time users see the /logout explainer before adding their first account.
  • Onboarding window grew from 460 → 620 px so the new card fits comfortably without forcing a scroll on standard displays.

item.title: Version 10.3
item.description:
• Daily background update checks are now enabled.
  • Stale "no public release pipeline" comment removed.
  • packaging/appcast.xml committed on main as feed source-of-truth.

item.title: Version 10.2
item.description:
• First release served through the Sparkle auto-update pipeline.
  • Sparkle EdDSA signing live; appcast hosted at the SUFeedURL.
  • Real public key embedded; signed zip enclosure.
